decisions.prompt: It's 6:30. The launch is shaky but might survive without you. Your evening is yours if you want it.

options.label: Stay until it's stable
options.flavor: You stayed until 10. The build went green at 9:47. You won't forget that moment.

options.label: Log off — they'll figure it out
options.flavor: You closed the laptop. The launch was fine without you. You wonder why you sometimes assume it won't be.

decisions.prompt: A recruiter from a company you've heard of is calling. You don't have to answer.

options.label: Take the call
options.flavor: Twenty minutes of pleasantries and a vague 'we should talk again.' Sometimes that's the whole point.

options.label: Let it go to voicemail
options.flavor: You don't return it. The week is full. The recruiter, presumably, calls someone else.

decisions.prompt: The bonus hit. The group chat wants Vegas. Your couch wants you to stay.

options.label: Go all in
options.flavor: You lost $1,500 and gained two stories you'll tell for a decade. Even trade.

options.label: Low-key — food and a show
options.flavor: You came home rested. The group chat called you boring. You don't mind.

options.label: Keep the bonus
options.flavor: The bonus stays where it is. Friday, you order in. Saturday, you don't think about work.

decisions.prompt: There's more work than there are hours. You can pour yourself into it for a stretch, or hold the line at forty.

options.label: Pour yourself in
options.flavor: You shipped two months early. People noticed. You haven't seen your friends since spring.

options.label: Hold the line
options.flavor: You walked out at five every day. The work got done. A little later than it could have.

decisions.prompt: The legacy module breaks again. You can patch it for the third time, propose a full rewrite, or just write up the debt.

options.label: Patch it. Again.
options.flavor: Twenty minutes. It works. You add a comment that says 'TODO: rewrite this someday.'

options.label: Pitch the rewrite
options.flavor: You got the green light. Three months of your life are now spoken for. Worth it, probably.

options.label: Write up the debt and move on
options.flavor: You filed the ticket. Someone, someday, will read it. Maybe that someone is you.

decisions.prompt: The on-call rotation is short a body. The lead is asking, gently.

options.label: Take the pager
options.flavor: You took two weeks. One quiet night, one rough one. The lead remembers.

options.label: Pass this round
options.flavor: You say no, politely. The lead understands, mostly. Someone else takes the pager.

decisions.prompt: The CFP closes Friday. You have a talk in you, probably. You also have a deadline.

options.label: Submit the abstract
options.flavor: You hit submit at 11:58 Friday. Acceptance or rejection — either way, the abstract exists.

options.label: Skip it this year
options.flavor: You closed the tab. The CFP closes. The deadline at work, mercifully, slips by a week anyway.

decisions.prompt: The standup is forty-seven minutes long. Three people have stopped speaking. The Slack message that could have replaced it would have been forty words.

options.label: Say something
options.flavor: You said 'Maybe we Slack the status next time?' A long pause. Then nods. The next standup was fourteen minutes.

options.label: Wait it out
options.flavor: Forty-seven minutes. You looked at your unread emails. The standup ended without resolution. As did the emails.

decisions.prompt: A cousin you haven't spoken to in two years messages: 'I have an opportunity I'd love to share with you.' You haven't scrolled down yet.

options.label: Hear them out
options.flavor: Forty-five minutes you'll never get back. The cousin sent a follow-up the next morning.

options.label: Politely decline
options.flavor: You typed three drafts. The one you sent said 'Not for me, but I hope it goes well.' They left you on read.

decisions.prompt: The gym is running a sign-on special. Forty dollars a month, no commitment. The membership is the easy part.

options.label: Sign up. You'll go.
options.flavor: You went four times in January. Twice in February. The membership renewed in August. You meant to cancel.

options.label: Not this year
options.flavor: You closed the tab. You'll start running. You'll definitely start running.

decisions.prompt: A small, urgent fix is ready. It is 4:50pm on a Friday. The deploy takes ten minutes. The rollback also takes ten minutes.

options.label: Ship it. It's tiny.
options.flavor: The rollback held. The weekend held, just barely. You wrote 'no Friday deploys' on a sticky note Monday.

options.label: Monday morning
options.flavor: The fix waited. The world continued. Monday at 10am, the deploy was uneventful.

decisions.prompt: A junior in a public forum posts a question you can answer in your sleep. Three other people have already answered. None of them helpfully.

options.label: Write a real answer
options.flavor: Twenty minutes of writing. A code sample. A diagram, even. The asker said 'thank you so much.' Two of the wrong answers got more upvotes.

options.label: Close the tab
options.flavor: You closed the tab. The internet continued.

decisions.prompt: It's 6:12pm Friday. The intern slacks: 'No rush — could you take a look at my PR before Monday?'

options.label: Review it now
options.flavor: You reviewed it. The intern was waiting. They thanked you and then immediately pushed three more commits.

options.label: Reply: 'Monday morning, first thing.'
options.flavor: You set the boundary. The intern said 'sounds good!' You realized too late that they had nothing else to do all weekend.

decisions.prompt: The down payment math finally works. The interest rate doesn't. You stare at the listing anyway.

options.label: Make the offer
options.flavor: You bought the place. The first month, you noticed every sound the house made. By the third, you didn't.

options.label: Keep renting, keep saving
options.flavor: The listing sold to someone else within the week. You looked at it on Zillow six months later, just to see.

options.label: Maybe this isn't your city
options.flavor: You closed the tab. You aren't sure where home is. You aren't sure that's a problem.

decisions.prompt: A college friend is getting married. Flight, hotel, rental, suit. The total math is uncomfortable.

options.label: Go
options.flavor: You danced with people you hadn't seen in five years. The friend remembered you came. They'll remember for a long time.

options.label: Send a gift and call that day
options.flavor: You sent the registry's nicest thing in your budget. The friend wrote a sweet note. Something, very quietly, shifted.

decisions.prompt: A friend mentioned a therapist. You opened the booking page three times this week. Forty-five minutes, a hundred and sixty dollars.

options.label: Book it
options.flavor: You went. You cried at something small and were surprised by it. You booked the next one before you left.

options.label: Maybe next month
options.flavor: You closed the tab. The thing that made you open it was still there. You'd try again soon. You said that last month, too.

decisions.prompt: There is a dog at the shelter with a particular face. You did not go to the shelter to adopt a dog.

options.label: Take her home
options.flavor: She slept on the couch the first night. The bed by the second. You weren't a dog person. Apparently, you are now.

options.label: Walk away
options.flavor: You walked out. You thought about her on the drive home. You thought about her for a week.

decisions.prompt: HR's email: you've hit the PTO cap. Use it or cash it out at sixty percent.

options.label: Take the time
scene: Sleep you didn't know you needed.
options.flavor: You took two weeks. You came back and didn't remember why anything felt urgent. That feeling lasted four days, then faded.

options.label: Cash it out
options.flavor: The deposit hit. You didn't take the time. Next year, you told yourself.

decisions.prompt: A coworker is fundraising for a cause that matters to them. The goal isn't yours, but you respect them.

options.label: Give what they asked
options.flavor: You donated the suggested amount. They sent a thoughtful thank-you. Five other coworkers gave after they saw your name.

options.label: Give a small amount
options.flavor: You gave twenty. They thanked you with the same warmth. You felt fine about it. Mostly.

options.label: Pass
options.flavor: You closed the link. Their goal was met three days later anyway.

decisions.prompt: Your parent's car needs a transmission. They mentioned it like it was nothing.

options.label: Wire the money
options.flavor: You sent it without asking. They tried to refuse. They didn't refuse hard.

options.label: Call. Just talk.
options.flavor: You called for an hour. They didn't bring the car up again. You both knew it was on the table.

decisions.prompt: An offer arrives in another city. Thirty percent more, full relocation. A different climate. Everyone you know is here.

options.label: Take it
scene: An unfamiliar key in a new door.
options.flavor: You moved. The first month was a checklist. The second was lonelier than expected. The third, you started to recognize a barista.

options.label: Negotiate remote
options.flavor: You wrote a careful email. They said no. You declined. The recruiter said, on a follow-up call, that they'd remember you.

options.label: Stay put
options.flavor: You said no. The recruiter understood, mostly. You wondered, briefly, if you'd regret it. The week ate the thought.

decisions.prompt: The check engine light has been on for six weeks. The mechanic gave you a number that ends in 'or you could just get something newer.'

options.label: Newer car
options.flavor: You drove it off the lot. It felt strange to not hear a rattle. By the second week, you'd already gotten a door ding.

options.label: Patch it. Pray.
options.flavor: The mechanic shook his head, very gently. The car ran for another year. You loved it more than you should have.

decisions.prompt: You have an idea. It's been bothering you for a month. You also have a weekend you could give to nothing.

options.label: Build it
options.flavor: By Sunday night, you had something that worked. By Tuesday, you'd lost interest. The repo is still public, with no commits since.

options.label: Give yourself the weekend
options.flavor: You did nothing on purpose. The idea was still there Monday. It would wait.

decisions.prompt: Your roommate's lease is up. You could keep splitting rent or take the whole place yourself.

options.label: Live alone
options.flavor: You signed the new lease. The silence was the best part. The silence was sometimes the loudest part.

options.label: Find a new roommate
options.flavor: You picked the second one. They were quieter than the last. But... they let their dishes 'soak' a little longer than you prefer.

decisions.prompt: A match wants coffee Thursday. The bio was witty. The pictures were three years old. Probably.

options.label: Go
options.flavor: You went. They were taller in person. The conversation was fine. You walked home thinking: maybe.

options.label: Reschedule indefinitely
options.flavor: You said 'next week' and meant 'never.' They got it. The app unmatched you eight days later.

decisions.prompt: The director pulls you aside. There's a tech-lead role opening, and a senior IC track. Both are yours if you want them.

options.label: Stay deep in the code
options.flavor: You took the senior IC. You kept your hands on the keyboard. People asked you 'when are you going to be a lead?' for two years.

options.label: Take the lead role
options.flavor: You took the title. The work changed. You spent more time in docs than in the editor. Sometimes you missed it.

decisions.prompt: The org is splitting your team. Two roles need filling: people manager and tech lead. The director is asking which you'd rather.

options.label: People manager
options.flavor: You took it. Your calendar filled with one-on-ones. You learned that the hardest part of management is being patient on Wednesday afternoons.

options.label: Tech lead
options.flavor: You took it. Your calendar filled with design reviews. The code you wrote got smaller and stranger. It was usually load-bearing.

decisions.prompt: The Master's program admits you. Part-time, two years, twenty thousand. The job won't pay for it. The job won't replace it, either.

options.label: Enroll
options.flavor: Two years of nights. You finished. You don't know yet if it mattered. You suspect it did, somewhere, quietly.

options.label: Decline
options.flavor: You said no. The job kept moving. You read three of the textbooks anyway.

decisions.prompt: A former colleague is starting something. They're asking you to be employee five. The salary is lower. The equity is paper.

options.label: Join
options.flavor: You joined. The first six months were exhilarating. You wrote a small piece of the codebase that will outlive most of the rest of it.

options.label: Stay put
options.flavor: You said no, warmly. The company shipped, or didn't — you heard secondhand. You wondered what employee-five-you would think of current-you.

decisions.prompt: A junior on the team is struggling, not in a way they can name. The lead suggests, gently, that you'd be good at this.

options.label: Commit to it
options.flavor: You did the weekly thirties. Six months in, they shipped something hard. They credited you. You credited them. Both were a little true.

options.label: Be too busy
options.flavor: You said your plate was full. The lead nodded. The junior found mentorship somewhere else. They mentioned it once, lightly.

decisions.prompt: Architecture review. Your design is right. A staff engineer disagrees, loudly. Three other people are on the call.

options.label: Defend it
options.flavor: You held the line. You were respectful. You were also, ten minutes in, clearly correct. The staff engineer sent a friendly message after.

options.label: Capitulate
options.flavor: You backed down to keep the meeting moving. Six weeks later, you were rewriting the part you'd been right about.

decisions.prompt: The company hackathon is a sponsored weekend. Sleep on the floor. Build something dumb. The CEO gives out the prize.

options.label: Go
scene: A standing ovation. For someone else.
options.flavor: You built something genuinely funny. You didn't win. You knew three people now you didn't know on Friday.

options.label: Take the weekend
options.flavor: You stayed home. You watched the winning demo on Slack Monday. You smiled. You didn't regret it.

decisions.prompt: A meeting goes sideways. Leadership is asking why the deadline slipped. There's a real reason. There's also a coworker you could name.

options.label: Name the coworker
options.flavor: You named them. Leadership accepted it. The coworker found out on Slack from a friend. Things were never quite the same.

options.label: Take the L
options.flavor: You said 'the scope grew faster than I tracked.' Leadership wasn't thrilled. The coworker, weeks later, said 'I owe you.' You said 'we're fine.'

decisions.prompt: The maintainer of a library you depend on asks if you'd take it over. They're burnt out. The community is two thousand users.

options.label: Take it
options.flavor: You merged your first PR as maintainer the same week. By month three, you understood why the last one wanted out. You kept going anyway.

options.label: Decline, gently
options.flavor: You wrote a kind email. They thanked you for the honesty. Someone else took it. The library kept working, mostly.
